title: Accessibility Best Practices

standards.aria: Use ARIA correctly to enhance, not replace, native semantics

keyPrinciples.title: Perceivable
keyPrinciples.description: Users must be able to perceive the information

keyPrinciples.title: Operable
keyPrinciples.description: Users must be able to operate the interface

keyPrinciples.title: Understandable
keyPrinciples.description: Users must be able to understand the information

keyPrinciples.title: Robust
keyPrinciples.description: Content must be interpretable by assistive technologies
requirements: Name, role, value for custom controls

commonIssues.problem: Screen readers can't announce field purpose
commonIssues.fix: Always associate labels with form controls using 'for' attribute

commonIssues.problem: Images are meaningless to screen readers
commonIssues.fix: Add descriptive alt text, or alt='' for decorative images
Add icons, text, or patterns alongside color

commonIssues.problem: Users can't access functionality without mouse
commonIssues.fix: Ensure all interactive elements are focusable and have visible focus states

commonIssues.problem: Users can't see where they are on the page
commonIssues.fix: Never remove outline without providing alternative focus style

commonIssues.problem: Focus can get trapped or lost
commonIssues.fix: Trap focus in modal, return focus on close, don't allow tabbing outside
Use aria-live regions for dynamic updates

forms: Required fields marked visually and with aria-required
modals: Focus returns to trigger on close
Modal has accessible name (title or aria-label)
images: Icons with meaning have accessible names
color: Color not sole indicator of meaning

testing: Use axe or similar automated testing
Test with keyboard alone (Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, Space, Arrows, Escape)
Test with screen reader (NVDA, VoiceOver, JAWS)
Test color contrast with tools (WebAIM, Colour Contrast Analyser)
